mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than returns following flat/rising FEDFUNDS (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals labor market deterioration consistent with an approaching recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical triggers of the 0.3pp UNRATE threshold, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signal robust near-term GDP momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone carries the same predictive power because retail sales can rise on credit expansion while housing starts confirm durable-goods commitment.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST momentum signal fails to outperform either single-series baseline model in out-of-sample Rank IC, or joint-positive-momentum periods produce forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS elevated), a decline in industrial production signals supply-side weakness compounding tight financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in stagflation risk; in easing or low-rate regimes the same INDPRO decline is absorbed without equity stress.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not followed by significantly more negative 5-day SPX returns than INDPRO declines during low-FEDFUNDS regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment reflects tightening labor demand and rising aggregate income, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while CPI inflation simultaneously remains above 3% year-over-year, markets price persistent restrictive policy with limited near-term easing prospects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as valuation multiples compress under sustained high real rates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions hold jointly, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is not superior to the FEDFUNDS-only signal at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery regimes, a rebound in housing starts signals renewed credit availability and consumer confidence, acting as a leading indicator of broader economic recovery that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; in late-cycle regimes the same rebound signals speculative excess rather than genuine recovery and loses predictive power.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases during recovery-labeled periods fail to produce 20-day forward returns significantly above unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one distinct recovery episode.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls over a two-month window signals a synchronized supply-and-labor contraction that historically precedes broader economic downturns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts individually.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-PAYEMS deceleration signal does not outperform a single-series INDPRO or PAYEMS deceleration model in out-of-sample Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or produces false-positive rates above 50%.
